---
title: "Gidon Frischkorn, Ph.D."
subtitle: "Psychological Methods · Cognitive Psychology · Psychometrics"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: images/Frischkorn_Gidon_small.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 12em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/GidonFrischkorn
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gidon-frischkorn-4913431b1/
    - icon: orcid
      text: "ORCID"
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5055-9764
    - icon: google-scholar
      text: "Google Scholar"
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=gpol3F4AAAAJ&hl=de
    - text: "BlueSky"
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/gidon-frischkorn.bsky.social
      rel: me
listing:
  - id: projects
    contents: projects/*.qmd
    type: grid
    grid-columns: 2
    fields: [title, subtitle, description]
    sort: "date desc"
    sort-ui: false
    filter-ui: false
---

I am Gidon Frischkorn, an SNF Ambizione Fellow at the [Cognitive Psychology Lab](https://www.psychology.uzh.ch/en/areas/nec/allgpsy.html) at the [University of Zurich](https://www.uzh.ch/en.html) and Lecturer and Research Associate for Statistics and Methods at the [University of Lucerne](https://www.unilu.ch/en/). I did my PhD at [Heidelberg University](https://www.psychologie.uni-heidelberg.de/arbeitseinheit/persoenlichkeit-und-diagnostik) under Dirk Hagemann, and was elected Fellow of the Psychonomic Society in 2024.

I work on measurement — specifically, on whether the things we measure in psychology actually track the cognitive processes they are supposed to. My research uses formal mathematical models to connect observable data (response times, accuracy, choices) to the underlying processes that produced them. Most of my empirical work is in working memory, processing speed, and attention, but the question running through all of it is the same: are we measuring what we think we are?

For me, a statistical model is a formal statement about how a cognitive process works. Fitting it to data is a way of asking whether a theoretical idea is actually coherent. That is why my work pairs formal models with targeted experimental designs — so both the theory and the data are on the hook for what conclusions are defensible.

## Research Projects

::: {#projects}
:::
